message:
fix: add missing etcd deployment step to kcp mission (#1978)

The previous operator-based mission installed etcd-druid but never
deployed an actual etcd instance — the RootShard referenced a
non-existent etcd endpoint. This caused the install to fail silently.

Changes:
- Replace etcd-druid with quickstart etcd (test Helm chart from
  kcp-operator repo) — simpler and matches kcp's own quickstart docs
- Fix etcd endpoint to http://etcd.default.svc.cluster.local:2379
- Add DNS setup step between FrontProxy and kubeconfig generation
- Install cert-manager via kubectl apply (matches kcp docs)
- Fix operator-docs URL to point to /main/ instead of stale /v0.4/
- Add kcp-operator repo URL to sourceUrls
- Add git to prerequisites (needed to clone kcp-operator for etcd)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,62 +6,61 @@
   "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
   "mission": {
     "title": "Install and Configure Kcp on Kubernetes (Operator-Based)",
-    "description": "kcp is a Kubernetes-like control plane designed for managing multiple isolated clusters, known as workspaces. The kcp-operator is the recommended way to deploy kcp in production: it offers more features than plain Helm charts and can actively reconcile missing or changed resources on its own. This mission installs kcp using the operator-based method, including cert-manager for TLS, etcd-druid for etcd management, and the kcp-operator for deploying RootShard, FrontProxy, and admin access.",
+    "description": "kcp is a Kubernetes-like control plane designed for managing multiple isolated clusters, known as workspaces. The kcp-operator is the recommended way to deploy kcp: it reconciles missing or changed resources automatically. This mission installs kcp using the operator-based method with cert-manager for TLS, a simple etcd deployment for storage, and the kcp-operator for deploying RootShard, FrontProxy, and admin access.",
     "type": "deploy",
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
         "title": "Install cert-manager",
-        "description": "Install cert-manager to handle TLS certificate lifecycle management for kcp components:
```bash
helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io
helm repo update
helm upgrade --install --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --version v1.18.2 --set crds.enabled=true --atomic cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager
```",
+        "description": "Install cert-manager to handle TLS certificate lifecycle management for kcp components:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.18.2/cert-manager.yaml
```
Wait for the pods to be ready before proceeding.",
         "commands": [
-          "helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io",
-          "helm repo update",
-          "helm upgrade --install --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --version v1.18.2 --set crds.enabled=true --atomic cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager"
+          "kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.18.2/cert-manager.yaml",
+          "kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment --all --namespace cert-manager --timeout=120s"
         ],
         "validation": {
           "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace cert-manager",
           "expected": "Running"
         },
         "rollback": [
-          "helm uninstall cert-manager --namespace cert-manager",
-          "kubectl delete namespace cert-manager"
+          "kubectl delete -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.18.2/cert-manager.yaml"
         ]
       },
       {
-        "title": "Install etcd-druid operator",
-        "description": "Install the etcd-druid operator from the Gardener OCI registry to manage etcd clusters for kcp:
```bash
helm install etcd-druid oci://europe-docker.pkg.dev/gardener-project/releases/charts/gardener/etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid --create-namespace --version v0.33.0
```",
+        "title": "Install kcp-operator",
+        "description": "Add the kcp Helm repository and install the kcp-operator:
```bash
helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator
```",
         "commands": [
-          "helm install etcd-druid oci://europe-docker.pkg.dev/gardener-project/releases/charts/gardener/etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid --create-namespace --version v0.33.0"
+          "helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts",
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator"
         ],
         "validation": {
-          "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace etcd-druid",
+          "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-operator",
           "expected": "Running"
         },
         "rollback": [
-          "helm uninstall etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid",
-          "kubectl delete namespace etcd-druid"
+          "helm uninstall kcp-operator --namespace kcp-operator",
+          "kubectl delete namespace kcp-operator"
         ]
       },
       {
-        "title": "Install kcp-operator",
-        "description": "Add the kcp Helm repository and install the kcp-operator:
```bash
helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade --install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator
```",
+        "title": "Deploy etcd",
+        "description": "Deploy an etcd instance for kcp storage. This uses the quickstart etcd chart from the kcp-operator repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp-operator /tmp/kcp-operator
helm install etcd /tmp/kcp-operator/hack/ci/testdata/etcd
```
This deploys etcd into the default namespace at `etcd.default.svc.cluster.local:2379`. For production, use etcd-druid with TLS — see https://docs.kcp.io/kcp/v0.30/setup/production/",
         "commands": [
-          "helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts",
-          "helm repo update",
-          "helm upgrade --install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator"
+          "git clone https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp-operator /tmp/kcp-operator",
+          "helm install etcd /tmp/kcp-operator/hack/ci/testdata/etcd"
         ],
         "validation": {
-          "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-operator",
+          "command": "kubectl get pods -l app=etcd-statefulset",
           "expected": "Running"
         },
         "rollback": [
-          "helm uninstall kcp-operator --namespace kcp-operator",
-          "kubectl delete namespace kcp-operator"
+          "helm uninstall etcd",
+          "rm -rf /tmp/kcp-operator"
         ]
       },
       {
         "title": "Create certificate issuer",
-        "description": "Create a self-signed certificate issuer for kcp TLS certificates:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Issuer
metadata:
  name: selfsigned
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  selfSigned: {}
EOF
```",
+        "description": "Create a self-signed certificate issuer for kcp TLS certificates:
```bash
kubectl create namespace kcp-system
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Issuer
metadata:
  name: selfsigned
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  selfSigned: {}
EOF
```",
         "commands": [
           "kubectl create namespace kcp-system",
           "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Issuer
metadata:
  name: selfsigned
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  selfSigned: {}
EOF"
@@ -76,9 +75,9 @@

-        "description": "Deploy the kcp RootShard custom resource with embedded cache. Replace 'kcp.example.com' with your actual hostname:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: RootShard
metadata:
  name: root
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  external:
    hostname: kcp.example.com
    port: 6443
  certificates:
    issuerRef:
      group: cert-manager.io
      kind: Issuer
      name: selfsigned
  cache:
    embedded:
      enabled: true
  etcd:
    endpoints:
      - http://etcd.kcp-system.svc.cluster.local:2379
EOF
```",

+        "title": "Configure DNS",
+        "description": "Point your hostname at the FrontProxy LoadBalancer IP. Get the external IP and create a DNS A record:
```bash
kubectl get svc frontproxy-front-proxy --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip}'
```
Create a DNS A record: `kcp.example.com` → the IP shown above. For local testing, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` instead.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get svc frontproxy-front-proxy --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip}'"

+      },
       {
         "title": "Generate admin kubeconfig",
         "description": "Create a Kubeconfig custom resource to generate admin credentials, then extract the kubeconfig from the resulting secret:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: Kubeconfig
metadata:
  name: kcp-admin
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  username: kcp-admin
  groups:
    - system:kcp:admin
  validity: 8766h
  secretRef:
    name: admin-kubeconfig
  target:
    frontProxyRef:
      name: frontproxy
EOF

kubectl get secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.data.kubeconfig}' | base64 -d > kcp-admin.kubeconfig
export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kcp-admin.kubeconfig
```",
